name: geminicommit
description: Free AI commit messages using Gemini, OpenRouter, Groq, Claude, DeepSeek, xAI, Ollama, or any OpenAI-compatible LLM

virtualWorkspaces.description: Commit Sage requires a local git repository to read diffs and create commits.

untrustedWorkspaces.description: Commit Sage generates commit messages from diffs in any workspace, but auto-commit and auto-push are disabled in untrusted workspaces.

enumDescriptions: Google Gemini (free tier via AI Studio)
OpenRouter (300+ models via single API key, includes free models)
DeepSeek (works without VPN in restricted regions)
Custom OpenAI-compatible endpoint (LM Studio, vLLM, llama.cpp, Together, Fireworks, etc.)
commitSage.provider.type.description: Select the AI provider for commit message generation

enumDescriptions: Custom language — specify the language name in the 'Custom Language Name' setting below
commitSage.commit.commitLanguage.markdownDescription: Language for generated commit messages. Some commit formats may have limited support for non-English languages — pick `custom` and set `#commitSage.commit.customLanguageName#` for any language not listed.

commitSage.commit.customLanguageName.markdownDescription: Language name to use when `custom` is selected as the commit language (e.g. *Ukrainian*, *Italian*, *Arabic*). The format template will be translated via LLM and cached in `.commitsage/translations.json`.

enumDescriptions: Use the text in 'Custom Instructions' verbatim instead of a built-in template.
commitSage.commit.commitFormat.markdownDescription: Format style for commit messages. Pick `custom` to use the free-form text from `#commitSage.commit.customInstructions#` as the entire prompt template (auto-enables `#commitSage.commit.useCustomInstructions#` when selected via the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus)).

commitSage.commit.useCustomInstructions.markdownDescription: Use custom instructions for commit message generation. **When enabled, `#commitSage.commit.customInstructions#` must not be empty.**

commitSage.commit.customInstructions.markdownDescription: Custom instructions for commit message generation. Required when `#commitSage.commit.useCustomInstructions#` is enabled.

commitSage.commit.commitlint.enabled.markdownDescription: Validate the generated message against the rules of the selected `#commitSage.commit.commitFormat#`, auto-fix mechanical violations and retry with the LLM when needed (up to `#commitSage.commit.commitlint.maxRetries#` times). Has no effect with the `custom` format and is switched off automatically when `custom` is selected.

commitSage.commit.commitlint.maxRetries.markdownDescription: Maximum number of validation+refinement cycles when `#commitSage.commit.commitlint.enabled#` is on.

commitSage.commit.commitlint.rulesPath.markdownDescription: Custom path to the commitlint rules file (e.g. `./config/commitlint.config.js`). Used by the `conventional` and `angular` formats. Leave empty to auto-discover `commitlint.config.{js,cjs,json,yml,yaml}` in the repository root.

enumDescriptions: The bundled static validator with per-format rule sets (no project code is executed).

The repo's own commitlint CLI from node_modules — exact parity with CI (presets, extends chains, plugins). Falls back to builtin when unavailable. Applies to commitlint-compatible formats only (conventional, angular, atom, karma, semantic, google).
commitSage.commit.commitlint.engine.markdownDescription: Which engine validates generated messages when `#commitSage.commit.commitlint.enabled#` is on.

commitSage.commit.onlyStagedChanges.markdownDescription:
Generate commit message only for staged changes.

- **Disabled (default):** commits staged changes if present, otherwise commits all tracked changes.
- **Enabled:** ignores unstaged work entirely.

commitSage.commit.autoCommit.markdownDescription: Automatically commit changes after generating the message. Consider disabling `#commitSage.commit.promptForRefs#` when using this feature.

commitSage.commit.autoPush.markdownDescription: Automatically push changes after committing. **Requires `#commitSage.commit.autoCommit#` to be enabled — ignored otherwise.**

commitSage.commit.promptForRefs.markdownDescription: Prompt for entering refs (e.g., issue numbers) when generating commit messages. **Note:** this may interrupt automatic flow if `#commitSage.commit.autoCommit#` is enabled.

commitSage.gemini.model.markdownDescription: Gemini model for commit message generation. The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus): it fetches Google's live model list and updates this setting on selection. Use `auto` to try all available models and prefer the best quality. Only used when Gemini provider is selected.

commitSage.codestral.model.markdownDescription: Codestral model for commit message generation. The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it fetches Mistral's live model list and updates this setting on selection. Only used when Codestral provider is selected.

commitSage.openai.model.markdownDescription: OpenAI model for commit message generation. The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it fetches OpenAI's live model list (filtered to chat-capable families) and updates this setting on selection. Only used when OpenAI provider is selected.

commitSage.openai.baseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL for OpenAI API. Change only if using Azure OpenAI or a custom endpoint. Default: <https://api.openai.com/v1>

commitSage.ollama.baseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL for Ollama API. Only used when Ollama provider is selected. Default: <http://localhost:11434>

commitSage.ollama.model.markdownDescription: Ollama model name. The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it fetches the live list from `/api/tags`. Make sure the model is installed locally. Only used when Ollama provider is selected.

commitSage.ollama.useAuthToken.description: Enable authentication token for Ollama API. Enable this if your Ollama instance requires an auth token

commitSage.ollama.numCtx.markdownDescription: Context window length passed to Ollama as `options.num_ctx`. **`0` (default) leaves the per-model Ollama default in place** — set explicitly only if your model's default is too small for your diffs (e.g. `8192`, `16384`, `32768`). Larger values use more RAM/VRAM.

commitSage.openrouter.model.markdownDescription: OpenRouter model ID (e.g. `meta-llama/llama-3.3-70b-instruct:free`, `anthropic/claude-haiku-4-5`, `deepseek/deepseek-chat`). The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it fetches OpenRouter's live model list. Only used when OpenRouter provider is selected.

commitSage.openrouter.preferFreeModels.markdownDescription: Filter the OpenRouter model picker to free models only (models with `:free` suffix or zero pricing). Default: show all 300+ models (free + paid). Enable to narrow the picker to free-tier only.

commitSage.groq.model.markdownDescription: Groq model ID (e.g. `llama-3.3-70b-versatile`, `llama-3.1-8b-instant`, `mixtral-8x7b-32768`). The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it fetches Groq's live model list. Only used when Groq provider is selected.

commitSage.anthropic.model.markdownDescription: Anthropic model ID (e.g. `claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929`, `claude-haiku-4-5-20251001`, `claude-opus-4-1-20250805`). See <https://docs.anthropic.com/en/docs/about-claude/models> for the current list. Only used when Anthropic provider is selected.

commitSage.deepseek.model.markdownDescription: DeepSeek model ID (e.g. `deepseek-chat`, `deepseek-reasoner`). The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it fetches DeepSeek's live model list. Only used when DeepSeek provider is selected.

commitSage.xai.model.markdownDescription: xAI Grok model ID (e.g. `grok-3-mini`, `grok-3`, `grok-4-fast`, `grok-4-1-fast`, `grok-code-fast-1`). The canonical UI is the [Commit Sage sidebar](command:commitsage.settings.focus) — it falls back to a static list if `/v1/models` is blocked (e.g. on fresh xAI accounts without credits). Only used when xAI provider is selected.

commitSage.custom.baseUrl.markdownDescription: Base URL of any OpenAI-compatible `chat/completions` endpoint. Examples: `http://localhost:1234/v1` (LM Studio), `http://localhost:8000/v1` (vLLM), `http://localhost:8080/v1` (llama.cpp server), `http://localhost:8080/v1` (LocalAI), `https://api.together.xyz/v1` (Together AI), `https://api.fireworks.ai/inference/v1` (Fireworks). Only used when Custom provider is selected.

commitSage.custom.model.markdownDescription: Model ID to send to the Custom endpoint. Free-form — depends on what your self-hosted server exposes (e.g. `qwen2.5-coder:7b`, `meta-llama/Llama-3.3-70B-Instruct-Turbo`). Only used when Custom provider is selected.

commitSage.custom.useApiKey.markdownDescription: Send an API key to the Custom endpoint. Disable for self-hosted servers that don't require auth (LM Studio, llama.cpp server default). When enabled, set the key via the `Commit Sage: Set Custom API Key` command.

commitSage.custom.chatCompletionsPath.markdownDescription: Path appended to `#commitSage.custom.baseUrl#` for chat completion requests. Default is `/chat/completions` — change only if your server uses a non-standard path.

commitSage.general.temperature.markdownDescription: LLM sampling temperature applied to all providers. Lower (e.g. `0.2`) = more deterministic / repeatable commit messages; higher (e.g. `1.0`) = more varied wording. Range `[0, 2]`. For Ollama it's threaded into `options.temperature`; for Gemini into `generationConfig.temperature`; for OpenAI-compatible providers (OpenAI/Groq/OpenRouter/DeepSeek/xAI/Codestral/Custom) and Anthropic — into the top-level `temperature` field.

commitSage.general.maxDiffSize.markdownDescription:
Maximum diff size in characters sent to the LLM. Larger diffs are truncated with a `...(truncated)` marker. **Set to `-1` to disable truncation (sends the entire diff — only safe with long-context models).** Clamped to the range `[1000, 1000000]`.

**Tighten this for low-TPM free tiers:**
- **Groq free tier (6000 TPM)** — set to ~`20000` (~5000 tokens for the diff + room for prompt/response).
- **OpenRouter free (20 RPM / 50 RPD)** — `40000` typically fine.
- **Gemini / Anthropic / DeepSeek paid** — default `100000` is fine.

Rule of thumb: 1 token ≈ 4 chars, so `100000` chars ≈ 25000 tokens.

commitSage.apiRequestTimeout.markdownDescription: Timeout for LLM provider HTTP requests (Gemini/OpenAI/Codestral/Ollama) in seconds. **Set to `-1` for no timeout (infinite wait).**

commitSage.gitTimeout.markdownDescription: Timeout for `git` subprocesses (diff, blame, push, etc.) in seconds. Push over a slow remote or diff over a huge file may need more than the default. **Set to `-1` for no timeout.**

scripts.test: npm run test:unit && npm run test:e2e
